entries.name: Scotch-Weld CA5
entries.summary: Slow-setting higher-viscosity instant adhesive for rough or uneven parts where you want a little alignment time before lockup.
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
entries.referenceForJoining: Composite, metal, plastic, rubber

entries.name: Scotch-Weld CA50 Gel
entries.summary: Surface-insensitive non-sag gel CA for vertical joints, porous parts, and larger bond gaps with more reposition time than fast liquids.
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl cyanoacrylate gel; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
entries.referenceForJoining: Composite, metal, plastic, rubber

entries.name: Scotch-Weld PR1500
entries.summary: High-viscosity plastic-and-rubber instant adhesive with good gap filling and less unwanted wicking into nearby seams.
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl hybrid cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
cautions: Use a suitable primer when bonding polyethylene or polypropylene.
entries.referenceForJoining: Metal, plastic, rubber

entries.summary: Low-viscosity high-purity CA aimed at difficult rubbers and hard-to-bond plastics without giving up fast production-line cure.
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
cautions: Prime polypropylene, polyethylene, PTFE, or silicone with Permabond POP.
entries.referenceForJoining: Composite, metal, plastic, rubber

entries.summary: High-viscosity CA for wire tacking and component bonding where you need fast fixture without aggressive wicking.
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
entries.referenceForJoining: Composite, metal, plastic, rubber

entries.summary: Large-gap high-viscosity CA that also behaves well on porous substrates where thin grades disappear too quickly.
entries.cureDetail: One-part ethyl cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
entries.referenceForJoining: Cardboard, composite, fabric, metal, plastic, rubber, wood

entries.summary: The classic methyl CA for fast metal bonding, with especially strong performance on steel, aluminum, and other engineering metals.
entries.cureDetail: One-part methyl cyanoacrylate; moisture-triggered room-temperature cure
cautions: Prime polypropylene, polyethylene, PTFE, or silicone with Permabond POP.
entries.referenceForJoining: Composite, metal, plastic, rubber

entries.name: Fusor 2098
entries.summary: Crash-durable 2-part structural epoxy for OEM-style weld bonding, rivet bonding, and replacement auto-body panel repairs.
entries.cureDetail: Two-component epoxy-based automotive structural adhesive; room-temperature cure with optional 60 C heat acceleration
cautions: Use only for trained collision-repair workflows and follow OEM panel replacement instructions.
Do not use adhesive-only bonding on structural components unless the vehicle manufacturer explicitly approves it.
entries.referenceForJoining: Steel, aluminum, e-coat, bare metals, FRP, and auto-body replacement panels

entries.name: Fusor 108B/109B
entries.summary: Medium-cure acrylic metal bonder for panel bonding, weld bonding, and rivet bonding with faster room-temperature cure than typical panel bonders.
entries.cureDetail: Two-component acrylic-based metal bonding adhesive with room-temperature snap cure
cautions: Use only for trained collision-repair workflows and follow OEM panel replacement instructions.
Do not use adhesive-only bonding on structural components unless the vehicle manufacturer explicitly approves it.
entries.referenceForJoining: Bare metal, steel, aluminum, quarter panels, roof panels, door skins, van side panels, and truck bed outer panels

entries.name: Fusor 112B
entries.summary: Slow-set acrylic metal bonder for larger panel replacements and hot-shop conditions where you need much longer open time before clamping.
entries.cureDetail: Two-component acrylic-based metal bonding adhesive with extended room-temperature work time
cautions: Use only for trained collision-repair workflows and follow OEM panel replacement instructions.
Do not use adhesive-only bonding on structural components unless the vehicle manufacturer explicitly approves it.
entries.referenceForJoining: Bare metal, steel, aluminum, quarter panels, roof panels, door skins, van side panels, and truck bed outer panels

entries.name: H20E
entries.summary: Two-part silver-filled conductive epoxy for chip bonding, die attach, and EMI shielding where both thermal and electrical conduction matter.
entries.cureDetail: Two-component 1:1 (by weight) silver-filled epoxy; recommended cure 150 C / 1 hour, alternative cures 120 C / 15 min or 80 C / 3 hours
cautions: TDS lists <300 C only as intermittent operating limit; no continuous service rating is specified.
Filled system; stir each component thoroughly before mixing.
Performance can shift if repackaged out of the original containers.
entries.referenceForJoining: Au, Ag, Cu, Si, GaAs die; Ag-plated and Au-plated lead frames; EMI/RF substrates

entries.name: OG198-54
entries.summary: Low-viscosity single-component UV-curable epoxy for active alignment of optics, fiber-to-V-groove bonding, and optoelectronic assembly with shadow-cure tolerance via thermal post-cure.
entries.cureDetail: Iron-doped mercury flood lamp 100 mW/cm^2 at 240-365 nm for >30 s; 365 nm LED flood or pulsed Hg lamp >30 s also qualified; full UV cure complete after 24 hours; thermal post-cure improves bond strength in shadow areas
cautions: Shadow areas need a secondary thermal post-cure to fully develop strength.
TDS lists <300 C only as intermittent operating limit; no continuous service rating is specified.
entries.referenceForJoining: Glass, optical fibers, V-grooves, optoelectronic hybrids, micro lenses

entries.name: EP30LTE-LO
entries.summary: Low-exotherm, low-viscosity two-part epoxy for cryogenic-through-elevated-temperature bonding, potting, and encapsulation, including spaceflight applications with NASA low outgassing compliance.
entries.cureDetail: 2-component room-temperature cure in 48-72 hours at 75 F; heat-accelerated cure 2-3 hours at 200 F; optimum cure is overnight at 75 F followed by 4-8 hours at 150-200 F
cautions: Two-part low-exotherm system; heat post-cure still recommended for optimum properties.
No lap-shear or thermal-conductivity value published on the product page; contact Master Bond for the full TDS.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Two-part liquid; Part A 35,000-70,000 cP, Part B 290-500 cP
entries.referenceForJoining: Composites, ceramics, metals, and dissimilar substrates for aerospace, optics, and spaceflight hardware

entries.name: UV15
entries.summary: Low-viscosity one-part epoxy-based cationic UV adhesive for bonding, sealing, and coating thin sections with very low shrinkage and high thermal stability.
entries.cureDetail: One-part UV cure at 320-365 nm (optimum 365 nm), typical dose 20-40 mW/cm^2; cures thin sections of a few microns up to 0.015-0.020 in in 15-30 seconds; optional 125 C / 30 min post-cure raises Tg
cautions: Cationic UV cure: shadow areas do not progress the way a radical-cure acrylate does; design for direct light access.
TDS lists service range as -80 F to +350 F; post-cure at 125 C for 30 minutes is recommended to reach the higher Tg.
entries.referenceForJoining: Glass, ceramics, optical components, thin-section potting, polymer optical waveguides

entries.name: Supreme 10AOHT
entries.summary: Heat-cured alumina-filled two-part epoxy paste for high-temperature thermal management bonds that still need electrical isolation, from cryogenic temperatures through +204 C.
entries.cureDetail: Heat cure: 250 F / 60-70 min or 300 F / 40-50 min
cautions: Alumina-filled system is thermally conductive but electrically insulative; volume resistivity >1e14 ohm-cm.
Heat cure is required; room-temperature cure is not the rated cure path on the product page.
entries.referenceForJoining: Heat sinks, power semiconductor packaging, thermally stressed electronics, and high-temperature sensor bonding

entries.name: Fusor 152
entries.summary: Medium-speed urethane plastic repair adhesive for bumper tabs, cosmetic fills, and larger plastic repairs that need a sandable feather edge.
entries.cureDetail: Two-component urethane-based plastic repair adhesive; room-temperature cure with optional 60 C heat acceleration
cautions: Structural plastic repairs require a backer patch for strength.
Use only for trained collision-repair workflows and verify substrate compatibility before full repair.
entries.referenceForJoining: All plastic bumpers, bumper tabs, SMC or fiberglass panels, e-coat, primed metals, and painted metals

entries.name: 353ND
entries.summary: Heat-cure two-part optical-grade epoxy widely used to seal optical fiber into ferrules and bond semiconductor packages; amber after cure with high refractive index and low outgassing.
entries.cureDetail: 10:1 by weight; recommended cure 150 C / 1 hour; alternative cures 150 C / 1 min, 120 C / 5 min, 100 C / 10 min, or 80 C / 30 min
cautions: Service temperature < 350 C is intermittent per TDS; continuous upper service is bounded by Tg >= 90 C.
Syringe packaging and crystallization in storage affect initial viscosity; warm the container until crystals disappear (Tech Tip #7).
Total mass should not exceed 25 grams during mixing to control exotherm.
entries.referenceForJoining: Optical fiber into ferrules (800-1550 nm), fiber-component packaging, semiconductor wafer-to-wafer bonding, flip-chip underfill

entries.name: H77
entries.summary: Heat-cure two-part thermally conductive, electrically insulating epoxy designed for lid-sealing hermetic micro-electronic packages; near-hermetic MEMS seals and UHV-compatible.
entries.cureDetail: 100:15 by weight; recommended cure 150 C / 1 hour; alternative 2-step 100 C / 1 hour then 120 C / 2 hours
cautions: Filled thermal system must be stirred thoroughly before mixing; settled filler will otherwise skew thermal and mechanical properties.
Service < 350 C is intermittent per TDS; Tg is only >= 80 C, so continuous-load service should stay well below that.
Passes ASTM E595 outgassing only with proper cure; abbreviated cures will not hit vacuum-grade performance.
entries.referenceForJoining: Hermetic lid-sealing of ceramic/metal/kovar packages, MEMS pressure sensors and accelerometers in TO-cans, optical filter window sealing

entries.name: EP42HT-2
entries.summary: Room-temperature curable two-part epoxy with exceptionally wide -60 F to +450 F service range; low exotherm castable up to 2-3 inches, clear/amber with good optical transmission.
entries.cureDetail: 100:40 by weight (or 100:50 by volume); 48-72 hours at 75 F, or 2-3 hours at 200 F; optimal is ambient cure plus 3-5 hour post-cure at 225-300 F
cautions: Room-temperature cure is rated but slow (48-72 hours); heat cure is required for the full strength and temperature range.
Castable up to 2-3 inches because exotherm is low for an epoxy, but large masses should still be monitored for peak temperature.
Hardness is rated > 75 Shore D; very rigid, not intended for flexible or impact-loaded joints.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 55,000-110,000 cP, Part B 30-70 cP; low-to-medium viscosity mixed
entries.referenceForJoining: Electronic, electrical, fiber-optic, optical and OEM applications with wide thermal excursions

entries.name: MasterSil 151
entries.summary: Two-part addition-cure optically clear silicone potting and encapsulation compound; 220-2,500 nm transmission with long pot life for LED and fiber-optic cladding applications.
entries.cureDetail: 10:1 by weight; 24-48 hours at 75 F or 1-2 hours at 200 F; 100% solids, no by-products released during cure
cautions: Addition-cure silicones are poisoned by amine, sulfur, tin, and some metal catalysts; qualify adjacent materials before potting.
Shore A 50-60 elastomer; not a structural adhesive, and peel-from-substrate is expected on smooth non-primed surfaces.
Optical transmission depends on bubble-free dispense; vacuum degas the mixed compound before potting optics.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 2,000-4,000 cP, Part B 50-150 cP; thin mixed consistency
entries.referenceForJoining: Encapsulating LEDs, optical fiber cladding, potting connectors, opto-electronic assemblies

entries.name: Supreme 11AOHT
entries.summary: Toughened thermally conductive, electrically insulating two-part epoxy with 1:1 mix and very wide -112 F to +400 F service range; good for bonding dissimilar substrates under thermal cycling.
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 by weight or volume; 8-12 hours at 75 F plus 1-2 hours at 150-200 F for optimum; 100 g open time ~20-30 minutes
cautions: TDS lists Shore D 80-85 after full cure; rigid enough to transmit stress into brittle substrates if the stack is poorly designed.
Part A 200,000-300,000 cP and Part B paste: meter-mix equipment needs the right ratio-drive for this viscosity combination.
Thermally conductive / electrically insulative balance holds only if the thick-section cure schedule is followed.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 200,000-300,000 cP, Part B paste; toothpaste-like mixed
entries.referenceForJoining: Electronic, electro-optic, aerospace, and specialty OEM assemblies under thermal cycling

entries.name: EP21TDCHT-LO
entries.summary: Toughened two-part epoxy with forgiving 1:1 mix, NASA low-outgassing pass, and -100 F to +350 F service range for bonding dissimilar substrates under thermal cycling.
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 by weight; overnight at room temperature followed by 2-3 hours at 200 F, or straight heat cure at 150-200 F for 2-3 hours
cautions: Passes NASA low-outgassing (ASTM E595) only when the recommended cure is followed; abbreviated schedules will not meet the requirement.
Low-outgassing designation is for vacuum/aerospace - general-purpose structural users do not need to pay for this grade.
Shore D 65-75 after cure: toughened but still rigid compared to flexible epoxies like E-120HP.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Part A 60,000-110,000 cP, Part B 70,000-140,000 cP; moderate viscosity paste
entries.referenceForJoining: Aerospace, electronic, vacuum, semiconductor, and specialty OEM assemblies needing low outgassing and thermal-cycling tolerance

entries.summary: General-purpose two-part thixotropic epoxy paste with forgiving 1:1 mix and flexible cure (23 C / 24 hr to 100 C / 30 min); high-viscosity pre-cure holds parts in place without fixtures.
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 by weight; recommended cure 80 C / 2 hours, alt 100 C / 30 min or 23 C / 24 hours
cautions: Service < 250 C is intermittent per TDS; Tg is only >= 55 C, so continuous-load service should stay at ambient to low-heat conditions.
Thixotropic paste is designed for limited flow; capillary-fill bonds should use a lower-viscosity EPO-TEK grade.
Weight loss at 300 C is 4.16% per TDS - not suited for vacuum/outgassing-sensitive applications.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Smooth paste, 80,000-120,000 cPs @ 23 C (2.5 rpm); thixotropic index 2.1
entries.referenceForJoining: Job shop / repair, outdoor and recreational bonding, SMD-to-PCB staking, LCD plug seal, X-ray sensor fabrication on lead, wood-working

entries.summary: High-Tg fiber-optic grade two-part epoxy with 1:1 mix and 24-hour pot life; NASA low-outgassing approved, suited for MEMS spin-coating and hermetic LCD glass seals.
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 by weight; recommended cure 150 C / 1 hour
cautions: 24-hour pot life is unusually long for an epoxy; mixed material still needs the 150 C / 1 hour heat cure to develop properties.
Passes ASTM E595 NASA outgassing only with the recommended 150 C / 1 hour cure.
Service < 300 C is intermittent per TDS; Tg >= 95 C bounds the continuous-load operating range.
entries.referenceForJoining: Fiber optic packaging, MEMS pressure/accelerometer spin coating, wafer-to-wafer CSP bonding, capillary flip-chip underfill, hermetic IR-filter sealing

entries.name: OG142-87
entries.summary: Single-component UV-cure optical epoxy for fiber-optic and optoelectronic packaging; >97% transmission 580-1660 nm, upgraded replacement for OG142-13 with better bonding and moisture resistance.
entries.cureDetail: Iron-doped mercury flood lamp 100 mW/cm^2 @ 240-365 nm for >30 sec; alt: Fe-Hg spot >90 sec, 365 nm LED flood >90 sec, pulsed Hg >60 sec; full cure 24 hours after exposure
cautions: UV cure requires direct line-of-sight to the adhesive; shadowed joints will not progress even with optional thermal post-cure.
Shelf life is only one year refrigerated, not ambient; refrigerated storage is mandatory.
Service < 300 C is intermittent per TDS; continuous-load service is bounded by Tg >= 100 C.
entries.referenceForJoining: Fibers in V-grooves, fiber/lens arrays, fiber splicing and coupling, beam-splitter cubes and prisms, 400-2000 nm active beam path

entries.name: T7109
entries.summary: Two-part thermally conductive epoxy for die attach and heat-sinking across semiconductor, hybrid, medical, and optical packages; low-stress alternative to EPO-TEK 930-4 with 0.7-1.5 W/mK.
entries.cureDetail: 10:1 by weight; recommended cure 150 C / 1 hour, alt 150 C / 10 min, 100 C / 4 hours, or 80 C / 8 hours
cautions: Thermal conductivity is geometry-dependent: 1.5 W/mK at 3 mil bond line, 0.7 W/mK at 40 mil; design the joint for the thin regime to get the headline number.
Filled thermal system must be stirred thoroughly before mixing; settled filler will skew thermal and mechanical values.
Service < 300 C is intermittent per TDS; Tg >= 45 C bounds continuous-load service well below the intermittent ceiling.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Smooth paste, 14,000-20,000 cPs @ 23 C (20 rpm); thixotropic index 1.8
entries.referenceForJoining: Semiconductor thermally conductive underfill, hybrid large die attach on ceramic, GaAs device attach, laser/photo-diode attach, micro-LCD die attach to flex or FR4

entries.name: ET5429
entries.summary: Thixotropic 2:1 two-part epoxy for composite assemblies with high shear and peel strength and 5 mm gap fill; tolerant of vertical joints without slumping.
entries.cureDetail: 2:1 by volume and weight; 2-4 hour pot life at 23 C; handling in 6-10 hours at 23 C, full cure 72 hours at 23 C or 2 hours at 60 C
cautions: Composite-focused grade; polyolefins and silicone/PTFE surfaces are out of scope.
Handling time is 6-10 hours at 23 C - parts must stay undisturbed or clamped for that long before moving.
Tg is only 50-60 C; hot-service use should stay below that for continuous loads.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Thixotropic paste; Part A 200,000 cP, Part B 60,000 cP @ 20 rpm
entries.referenceForJoining: Composite-to-composite, composite-to-metal, FRP, carbon fiber, automotive and marine assemblies

entries.name: TA4605
entries.summary: Two-part 1:1 toughened methacrylate that bonds polyolefins (PP, PE, HDPE) without primer or surface treatment; room-temperature full cure in 24 hours with 5-10 minute fixture time.
entries.cureDetail: 1:1 ratio; pot life 2-4 minutes, fixture time 5-10 minutes, handling strength at 20-25 minutes, working strength 2-4 hours, full cure 24 hours at 23 C
cautions: Pot life is 2-4 minutes at 23 C; this is a fast structural acrylic, not a spread-on epoxy.
Adhesive has a strong acrylic odor - use ventilation especially when dispensing more than a small bead.
PTFE-level surface energy is the lower bound; LSE plastics like silicone and fluoropolymers will still need a primer.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Thixotropic paste; Part A 150,000-400,000 cP, Part B 20,000-30,000 cP
entries.referenceForJoining: Polypropylene, polyethylene, HDPE, ABS, polycarbonate, PVC, and dissimilar plastics-to-metals bonding

entries.name: TA4590
entries.summary: No-mix urethane-methacrylate structural acrylic with 15-30 second fixture time on steel using Initiator 44; non-corrosive to copper for sensor and electrical assemblies.
entries.cureDetail: 10:1.5 adhesive to initiator; 15-30 sec fixture on steel at 23 C, handling strength 30-60 sec, working strength 2-3 min, full cure 24 hours at 23 C
cautions: Designed for Initiator 44; do not substitute initiators from other manufacturers - the accelerator chemistry is matched.
Non-corrosive to copper parts is a distinguishing feature vs. other acrylics; preserve this by not using acidic primers.
Max gap fill is 0.5 mm; designs with larger gaps should switch to a 1:1 structural acrylic.
entries.referenceForJoining: Steel, ferrites, ceramics, copper parts, motors, sensors, and electronic assemblies needing fast fixture

entries.name: HM162
entries.summary: Medium-viscosity anaerobic retainer for cylindrical parts with high strength and excellent temperature resistance; 5 minute handling time and 24 hour full cure on steel.
entries.cureDetail: One-part anaerobic; metal-catalyzed room-temperature cure; handling strength 5 min on steel, working strength 1-3 hours, full cure 24 hours at 23 C
cautions: Cures only when confined between mating metal surfaces; will not polymerize on exposed beads.
Oxidized or passivated surfaces (stainless steel) cure slower; use A905 or ASC10 activator to normalize cure time.
Max gap fill is 0.2 mm - larger clearances need HH167 or a sleeved repair.
entries.referenceForJoining: Cylindrical fits, bearings, bushings, sleeves on shafts, rotors on motor shafts

entries.name: HH167
entries.summary: High-viscosity gap-filling silver anaerobic paste for repairing worn shafts, keyways, and splines up to 0.5 mm gap; room-temperature cure with 24 hour full strength on steel.
entries.cureDetail: One-part anaerobic paste; metal-catalyzed room-temperature cure; handling strength 15 min on steel, working strength 3-6 hours, full cure 24 hours at 23 C
cautions: Will not cure outside of the joint - TDS specifically calls out that exposed beads do not polymerize.
Gap fill rated only to 0.5 mm; beyond that the joint will never fully cure in the center.
Silver metallic paste is cosmetic, not electrically conductive in the usual thermal-conductivity sense - cite TDS for project-specific claims.
entries.referenceSampleConsistency: Silver paste; 500,000 cP @ 2 rpm, 90,000 cP @ 20 rpm
entries.referenceForJoining: Worn shafts, broken threads, damaged keyways and splines on ferrous machinery parts
